title: clypeola pyrenaica
intro: Clypeola pyrenaica, also known as Shepherd's Needle, is a flowering plant species in the family Brassicaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Clypeola pyrenaica is believed to have several health benefits, such as reducing fever and relieving digestive issues, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. The plant is often used to make herbal preparations, including infusions and tinctures, which are consumed for their perceived medicinal benefits.
